text: L-Arginine, chemical formula C6H14N4O2, molecular weight 174.20, is a pure organic molecular crystal without solvent or hydrate. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group P21 (No. 4), exhibiting piezoelectric properties suitable for energy harvesting applications. The crystal structure was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction (CCDC 855058, CSD refcode TAQBIY). Theoretical density functional theory calculations predicted a maximum piezoelectric voltage constant of gij approximately 274 mV m/N with a Young's modulus of 17.1 GPa, significantly exceeding piezoelectric ceramics like KNN and organic polymers like PVDF. The high piezoelectric response is attributed to continuous directional hydrogen bonding along the crystallographic axis and a favorable monoclinic angle of 97.4 degrees. L-Arginine exhibits eight non-zero piezoelectric tensor components with piezoelectric strain constants ranging from d14 approximately 0.28 pC/N to d16 approximately 6.52 pC/N. The predicted dielectric constant of 2.54 results in five voltage constants exceeding 40 mV m/N, making L-Arginine a promising eco-friendly energy harvesting material with low environmental impact and minimal processing costs compared to conventional piezoelectrics.
property_ref_title: Ab-Initio Predictions of the Energy Harvesting Performance of L-Arginine and L-Valine Single Crystals
